sourceClaimAudited.summary: A teammate report suggested applying Tao-van Doorn Appendix A, Theorem 16 to replace Sawhney Lemma 2.1/2.2 tails and possibly lower the explicit threshold to around 10^6 or below the existing 1..40500 exact evidence.
sourceClaimAudited.proposedConsequence: If the theorem directly bounded the relevant union/hitting sets with a Q^4/N penalty, the remaining exact finite range might shrink dramatically.

primarySourcesChecked.title: Growth rates of sequences governed by the squarefree properties of its translates
primarySourcesChecked.relevantResult: Appendix A, Theorem 16 / arithmetic large sieve for square moduli: a set avoiding omega(p^2) residue classes modulo p^2 satisfies |A| <= (N + Q^4) / sum_{q <= Q} h(q).

primarySourcesChecked.title: On A subset [N] such that ab+1 is never squarefree for a,b in A
primarySourcesChecked.author: Mehtaab Sawhney
primarySourcesChecked.relevantUse: Lemma 2.1 and Lemma 2.2 upper-bound union/hitting sets: elements lying in at least one square-obstruction residue class.

directionAudit.mismatch: The theorem upper-bounds the complement/avoiding side. Sawhney's weakest-branch bottleneck needs an upper bound on the union/hitting side. An upper bound on the avoiding side does not by itself produce the needed upper bound on the union.

numericalSanityCheck.method: Local Node one-off computation of sum_{q<=Q} h(q) for squarefree q, using omega(p^2)=2 for p == 1 mod 4, p >= 13 in the A* branch and omega(p^2)=1 for p != 2,5 in the Lemma 2.2-style branch.
numericalSanityCheck.interpretation: The large-sieve denominator is near 1 for the A* residue system, so the theorem cannot plausibly give a density bound near 1/25 from Q around 50 to 100. The proposed threshold-collapse arithmetic appears inverted.

repoImpact.reason: The reference is real and useful background, but the direct threshold-collapse route is blocked by direction-of-inequality and denominator-size checks.
futureUseIfAny: Look for a dual lower bound for the avoiding side or a separate lower-bound sieve that can upper-bound the union.
Check whether a different square-moduli sieve result gives the needed union/hitting upper bound with explicit constants.
Keep the current theorem-facing p4217/frontier abstraction lane active unless a corrected analytic threshold packet is produced.

proofBoundary: This audit only blocks the direct use claimed in the teammate report. It does not rule out a corrected analytic threshold route using a dual sieve, a different large-sieve inequality, or an explicit lower bound for the avoiding complement.
